# PikoCI

> PikoCI is a self-hosted, portable CI/CD system. One binary, any database, any queue, runs anywhere.

PikoCI is an open-source CI/CD system inspired by Concourse CI, built around a resource/resource-type pipeline model. It runs as a single binary with pluggable database (SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL, in-memory) and queue (NATS, Kafka, RabbitMQ, in-memory) backends.

## Key Features

- Single binary: download and run, no external dependencies required
- Portable: bundle the binary with a pipeline config and SQLite file, move anywhere
- HCL pipelines: Terraform-style syntax for defining CI/CD pipelines
- Resource model: pipelines built from resources and resource types
- Pluggable runners: run jobs on the host machine, in Docker containers, or custom runners
- Services: ephemeral processes (databases, caches) that run alongside job tasks
- Secret backends: pluggable secret resolution (file-based, env-based)
- Public pipelines: share pipeline status without requiring authentication
- Local execution: `pikoci run` executes pipeline jobs locally without a server
- Built-in web UI: visualize pipelines, stream build logs, manage configuration

## Links

- Website: https://pikoci.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/PikoCI/pikoci
- Documentation: https://github.com/PikoCI/pikoci/wiki
- Live instance: https://ci.pikoci.com
- License: Apache 2.0
